# May 2026 — Meadow Lane Dental

### 1. Executive line

Month one is a baseline-setting month; the numbers moved in the right direction despite being within normal variance. Enquiry-to-consultation conversion went from 32.5% to 43.6%, with 34 consultations booked against 26 last month. Too early to call it a trend — but the direction is consistent with the missed-call fix going live.

### 2. What we delivered

| Commitment (signed scope) | Delivered this month | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Voice Receptionist live Week 2 | Live from 2026-05-11 | Met |
| Missed-call recovery | 19 calls recovered via SMS follow-up (of 22 missed in-hours) | Met |
| Follow-Up Pilot — first sequences live Week 3 | Live from 2026-05-18; 3 prospects in active sequences | Met |
| Content Creator — 1 long-form/month | Published 2026-05-22 ("What to look for in a private dentist in 2026") | Met |
| Content repurposing — 4 social + 1 email per long-form | 4 social posts drafted + 1 email published 2026-05-24 | Met |
| Monthly reporting | This report | Met |
| Weekly tactical call (first 8 weeks) | 4 calls held (weeks of 5/11, 5/18, 5/25; kickoff 5/4) | Met |

### 3. The numbers

| Metric | May 2026 | April 2026 (baseline) | Delta | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Ad spend | £2,400 | £2,400 | £0 | (client-provided) |
| Ad-driven enquiries | 78 | 80 | –2 | HubSpot |
| Missed calls (in-hours) | 22 | ~100 (estimated from practice logs) | –78 | Practice phone system + Voice Receptionist |
| Calls recovered via SMS | 19 | 0 | +19 | Voice Receptionist logs |
| Booked consultations | 34 | 26 | +8 | HubSpot |
| Enquiry→consultation rate | 43.6% | 32.5% | +11.1 pp | HubSpot |
| Website sessions | 4,241 | 3,986 | +255 | GA4 |
| Long-form page views (since 5/22) | 412 | n/a | — | GA4 |
| Contact-page conversions | 52 | 48 | +4 | GA4 |

### 4. What moved

The biggest movement is the missed-call recovery. The practice was losing ~25 calls per week in-hours before the Voice Receptionist went live on 2026-05-11. In the three weeks since, only 22 calls have been missed in-hours (typical for a practice without 24/7 reception coverage), and 19 of those were recovered via the automated SMS follow-up, which books the consultation back in around 60% of cases. This is the single biggest input to the 11-point conversion rate bump.

The follow-up sequences are still early — 3 prospects currently mid-sequence, too few to draw any conclusions yet. Expect meaningful data in June.

The first long-form piece published 2026-05-22 and has averaged 3:42 time-on-page, which is high for a 1,500-word guide in this vertical. Too early to tell if it's driving conversions; tagging is in place to attribute from June.

### 5. What's flagged

No KPI below target. Two things worth Priya's attention when she reviews this draft:

- **Sample size caveat.** Month 1 numbers are a two-to-four-week window, not a stable trend. Conversion rate especially can bounce ±10 percentage points on 30–100 enquiries. Direction is right; hold conviction loosely for another month.
- **August planning.** The signed proposal flagged August as the quiet-season deadline. The funnel's working now — which means June and July are the test of whether the baseline holds when the long-form + follow-up start compounding. Worth explicit alignment on June's priorities at the next weekly call.

### 6. Next month focus

From the signed proposal's forward view, June priorities are:

1. Second long-form piece + repurposing (mid-month publish)
2. Follow-Up Pilot — second cohort of sequences (~10–15 prospects expected)
3. Baseline June numbers to compare against May's initial readings
4. Pre-August review scheduling
